input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI ContentPage called SettingsPage.xaml that currently uses
plain bindings with no x:DataType. The page's BindingContext is a
SettingsViewModel with properties: string Theme, bool NotificationsEnabled,
and int FontSize. Convert this page to use compiled bindings and tell me
how to catch binding errors at build time.

data.content: No existing files found — I'll create a demo showing exactly what to change.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.SettingsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Theme" />
        <Entry Text="{Binding Theme}" />

        <Label Text="Enable Notifications" />
        <Switch IsToggled="{Binding NotificationsEnabled}" />

        <Label Text="Font Size" />
        <Label Text="{Binding FontSize, StringFormat='Current size: {0}'}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>

</ContentPage>

data.content:
Here's what you need to do — two changes turn on compiled bindings:

### 1. Add `x:DataType` to the root element

```xml
<ContentPage ...
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel">
```

That's it for the XAML. The `x:DataType` attribute tells the XAML compiler the type of the `BindingContext`, so every `{Binding Theme}`, `{Binding NotificationsEnabled}`, etc. is resolved **at compile time** instead of via reflection at runtime.

### 2. Catch errors at build time

Once `x:DataType` is set, **misspelled or missing properties become build errors** automatically. For example, `{Binding Themee}` would produce:

```
error XC0045: Binding: Property "Themee" not found on "SettingsViewModel"
```

To make this even stricter, add this to your `.csproj`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <MauiStrictXamlCompilation>true</MauiStrictXamlCompilation>
</PropertyGroup>
```

This promotes XAML warnings to errors so nothing slips through.

### Key rules to remember

| Scenario | What to do |
|---|---|
| Binding to a different type mid-tree (e.g. inside a `DataTemplate`) | Add `x:DataType` on that element |
| Need to opt out for one binding (e.g. `RelativeSource`) | Use `x:DataType="x:Null"` on that element |
| ViewModel namespace lives in another assembly | Use `xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:…;assembly=…"` |
